understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by placing all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with `+`, ensuring that every `+` was simultaneously occupied by a selectable piece to trigger a WIN and progress through all levels.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controlled the currently selected piece shown as `P` (with other selectables as `S`). Movement actions attempted to shift the selected piece by one 3-pixel cell in the chosen direction (x increases right, y increases down), but could be blocked by walls/boundaries or objects. Some adjacent objects (e.g., blocks) could potentially be pushed if directly in the movement direction, moving together with the selected piece when space allowed. Selection could be switched at any time using `CLICK` on the exact pixel/grid coordinate of another selectable; this changed control without moving pieces. The movement-blocked flags were only hints indicating nearby objects and did not guarantee success or failure.
